text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic myomectomy, injecting diluted vasopressin into the fibroid capsule to facilitate aqua dissection. The fibroid is visibly released from the uterine cavity and bed.

The surgeon sutures the large bed of the anterior intramural fibroid using a multilayered approach with a quill suture, employing ipsilateral baseball sutures to obliterate the myoma cavity and achieve hemostasis. The suturing technique effectively prevents hematoma formation at the myoma bed.

The surgeon completes suturing the external layer of the myoma bed using baseball sutures, ensuring the external surface overlaps to minimize contact with the peritoneum and reduce adhesion risk. Gentle traction is applied to approximate the tissue along the suture line, with uniform tension provided by the quill suture for optimal healing. The sutured myoma bed appears bloodless at the end of the procedure.
